channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: IG Defence Unveils ‘IG JWALA’ Short-Range Missile System to Boost India’s Indigenous Strike Capabilities
item.description: India’s defence technology ecosystem continues to expand with private sector innovation, as Defencetech startup IG Defence has confirmed plans to develop the IG JWALA Short-Range Missile System. Positioned as a rapid-response, high-precision battlefield weapon, IG JWALA reflects a growing shift toward indigenous, agile strike systems designed for modern warfare scenarios.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army Floats AK-103 Upgrade Tenders for Holographic Sights, IR Lasers, Suppressors and Tactical Kits Under Make in India Push
item.description: The Indian Army has initiated a significant modernization drive for its AK-103 assault rifles, with a series of tenders released through the Government e-Marketplace (GeM) and Central Public Procurement Portal (CPPP). These procurements, aligned with the Make in India framework, are aimed at upgrading frontline infantry and Special Forces units with advanced optics, targeting systems, suppressors, and modular weapon furniture—collectively transforming the AK-103 into a more versatile and combat-adaptable platform.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Evaluates Russian UPAB-1500 Glide Bomb for Su-30MKI to Boost Long-Range Precision Strike Capability
item.description: India is reportedly evaluating the induction of Russian-origin UPAB-1500-class precision-guided glide munitions for integration with its Su-30MKI fighter fleet. The move is aimed at significantly enhancing the Indian Air Force’s long-range stand-off strike capability, particularly against hardened and high-value targets.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: TEDBF Design Frozen as ADA Awards High-Fidelity Wind Tunnel Contracts to NAL for Final Aerodynamic Validation
item.description: India’s Twin Engine Deck Based Fighter (TEDBF) program has reached a decisive milestone with the Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) effectively freezing the aircraft’s aerodynamic configuration. This transition marks the shift from conceptual design to physically validated engineering, as high-fidelity wind tunnel testing contracts have been awarded to the National Aerospace Laboratories (NAL) in Bengaluru.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL Rudra Spotted with Upgraded Dhruvastra HELINA ATGM
item.description: Recent imagery of Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) developed Rudra, the weaponised variant of the Advanced Light Helicopter (ALH-WSI), has revealed a significant enhancement in its anti-armour capability. The helicopter was observed carrying the indigenous Dhruvastra, also known as HELINA (Helicopter-launched Nag), in a tri-tube launcher configuration, with three missiles mounted on a single pylon.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Air Force’s Hovering Hawks Celebrate 40 Years of Glorious Service with 100% Fleet Serviceability
item.description: The Indian Air Force is proudly celebrating a remarkable milestone as the 132 Helicopter Unit, famously known as The Hovering Hawks, completes 40 years of dedicated and selfless service to the nation in 2026. This elite rotary-wing unit has etched its name in the annals of IAF history through its unmatched professionalism, operational excellence, and unwavering commitment to duty.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Emerges as a Key Battleground in Regional Aviation Push as Civil C-295 Variant Is Discussed
item.description: India’s regional aviation market is quietly emerging as a new battleground for global aircraft manufacturers, with multiple players positioning themselves to tap into short-haul routes that connect smaller cities, islands, and difficult terrain. Russia has already made its pitch by offering the Ilyushin Il-114 and the SSJ-100 for regional operations, while Brazil’s Embraer has moved aggressively by tying up with the Adani Group to explore opportunities in the same space.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: BrahMos Deals Deepen India’s Role In Indo-Pacific Security
item.description: As India seeks to position Southeast Asia as a key market for the BrahMos missile, the supersonic cruise weapon – jointly developed by India and Russia – has generated significant interest in the region amid growing Chinese assertiveness in the South China Sea.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India To Scale Nuclear Capacity To Reach 100 GW By 2047: Report
item.description: India has set a major target to scale up its nuclear power capacity to at least 100 GW by 2047, marking the centenary of its independence, as it looks to strengthen long-term energy security and reduce dependence on fossil fuels, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian-American Amit Kshatriya, senior NASA official behind US’ moon mission
item.description: From admiring rocket launches as a child growing up in Houston to steering the operations at the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), Indian-American scientist Amit Kshatriya has had a stellar innings at the space agency that has set on a mission to land astronauts on the moon.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Islamic State Playbook Focuses On COTS Drones To Maximise Psychological Impact
item.description: Lone actor terrorists are increasingly using drones to carry out reconnaissance missions of potential targets. Literature that is regularly posted by the various media wings of the Islamic State has been encouraging lone-actor terrorists to use drones to get information about the targets.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: I Don’t See Any Competition With Pak: Tharoor Dismisses Oppn’s Concerns On West Asia, Backs India’s Policy
item.description: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor on Saturday defended India’s approach to the ongoing West Asia conflict, stating that New Delhi is playing a “very good” role by actively engaging with regional leaders, while rejecting the Opposition’s allegations that Pakistan’s emerging role as a mediator reflects poorly on India’s foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Mumbai Police Uncover Major Terror Funding Case, Key Accused Allegedly Created 40 Fake Passports
item.description: In a significant breakthrough, the Mumbai Police have uncovered a major terror funding angle linked to a suspected hawala racket, with key accused Salim Sheikh allegedly creating fake passports for nearly 40 individuals using forged documents.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Another 312 Indian Fishermen Safely Evacuated From Iran To India Through Armenia: EAM Jaishankar
item.description: External Affairs Minister, S. Jaishankar, on Saturday said that another 312 Indian fishermen were safely evacuated from Iran to India through Armenia. He further thanked the government of Armenia for making it possible.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Box fitted with two grenades recovered in Rajouri, area cordoned off
item.description: Security forces on Friday recovered a suspected IED-type box containing two grenades in Jammu and Kashmir’s Rajouri district, prompting authorities to cordon off the area, officials said. Locals spotted suspicious material in the Thandipani area of the Sunderbani belt, following which security personnel rushed to the spot and secured the area, they said.
item.dc:creator: admin
